input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Behaviors>
        <toolkit:EventToCommandBehavior
            EventName="SelectionChanged"
            Command="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}" />
    </CollectionView.Behaviors>

    <RefreshView
        IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
        Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}">
        <!-- Wrap CollectionView if using RefreshView pattern -->
    </RefreshView>

</CollectionView>
```

A cleaner, more common pattern — **wrap the CollectionView in a `RefreshView`**:

```xml
<RefreshView
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="›" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // Must set false to stop spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null; // Reset so the same item can be re-selected
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `ObservableCollection<T>` | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | `MyItem?` | Tracks selection; set to `null` after navigation |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | — | Limits to one selection at a time |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Triggers data reload on pull-down |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` | `bool IsRefreshing` | Controls the spinner; **you must set it `false`** when done |

**Important notes:**
- **`IsRefreshing = false`** — if you forget this, the spinner never stops.
- **`SelectedItem = null`** — without this, tapping the same item twice won't re-trigger navigation.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`).
- `SelectionChangedCommand` is available natively on `CollectionView` in .NET MAUI 8+. For older versions, use `EventToCommandBehavior` from the MAUI Community Toolkit.
